title: clusia criuva subsp. ildefonsiana
intro: Clusia criuva subsp. ildefonsiana, also known as the Ildefonsiana clusia, is a plant species of the genus Clusia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The Ildefonsiana clusia is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are often used to treat various ailments in traditional folk medicine.
